137	### Citation discipline (the hard rule)
138	
139	**Every declaration block that derives from external reference material requires three citation elements:**
140	
141	1. **`% SOURCE:` LaTeX comment** — pointer + local file. Format: `% SOURCE: <citation pointer> (read from references/<file>.md)`. The pointer is the source identifier + section/theorem/definition number + page when available (e.g. `[Hartshorne], III.5.1, p. 174` or `[Stacks Project], Tag 01V4`). The `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical names the local file you opened to produce the verbatim quote.
142	2. **`% SOURCE QUOTE:` LaTeX comment** — the **verbatim text** of the cited statement, copied character-by-character from the local reference file you just named in the `% SOURCE:` line. Verbatim means:
143	   - **Original language.** French for Bourbaki / EGA, German for Grothendieck's early notes, English for Hartshorne / Vakil / Stacks, etc. Do NOT translate.

205	## Mathlib dependency anchors (`\mathlibok`)
206	
207	When your chapter's proofs rely on a definition or lemma that **Mathlib already provides** (and the project will not re-prove), it is good practice — not just a bare `\uses{}` to nowhere — to make that dependency a first-class blueprint block: a **Mathlib dependency anchor**. Doing so:
208	
209	- makes the reliance explicit and readable (a mathematician sees exactly which Mathlib result the route stands on);
210	- gives `leandag` a real node, so the `\uses{}` resolves (no broken/unknown ref) and the node's effort is **done** rather than ∞;
211	- keeps it clear how much the route leans on Mathlib vs. on original work.
212	
213	Write the anchor like any statement block, with three differences:
214	
215	```latex
216	\begin{lemma}[Hensel's lemma]
217	  \label{lem:hensel_mathlib}
218	  \lean{Mathlib.RingTheory.Henselian.foo}   % the REAL Mathlib declaration
219	  \mathlibok
220	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
221	  Statement of the result in the project's notation.
222	\end{lemma}
223	```
224	
225	- **`\lean{}` names the actual Mathlib declaration** (with its real namespace), so `leandag` matches the node to Mathlib.
226	- **`\mathlibok`** marks it as supplied by Mathlib — the DAG treats it as done; no `\begin{proof}` is needed.
227	- No `% SOURCE:`/`% SOURCE QUOTE:` block — Mathlib is the source; the `\lean{}` target *is* the citation.
228	
229	**The anti-hallucination rule (mirrors citation discipline):** mark `\mathlibok` ONLY when the statement genuinely exists in Mathlib and your stated form is faithful to it. A wrong or invented `\mathlibok` is worse than an ∞ hole — the loop will treat a non-existent result as available and skip proving a real gap. So:
230	
231	- Prefer anchors the **directive names** (the plan agent/reviewer identified them as Mathlib results).
232	- If you are not confident the exact statement is in Mathlib, do **not** mark `\mathlibok` — write the block without it (or leave the `\uses{}` for the reviewer to resolve) and flag it under "Notes for Plan Agent" so the reviewer can verify against Lean.
233	- Never put `\mathlibok` on one of the project's **own** to-be-proved declarations. That marker is for Mathlib-supplied results only; `\leanok` (project proofs) is never yours to add.

44	\begin{lemma}
45	\label{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}
46	Let $X$ be a scheme.
47	Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
48	Let $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$ be a standard
49	open covering of an affine open of $X$.
50	Then $\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for
51	all $p > 0$.
52	\end{lemma}

54	\begin{proof}

57	which generate the unit ideal of $A$.

59	Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
60	$\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
61	is identified with the complex
62	$$
63	\prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
64	\prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
65	\prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to

67	$$

70	\label{equation-extended}

72	M \to
73	\prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
74	\prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
75	\prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
76	\ldots
77	\end{equation}
78	(whose truncation we have studied in
79	Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact.
80	It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
81	is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
82	Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}.
83	In fact we will show that the extended complex localized

417	/-- **{\v C}ech cohomology transports along an isomorphism of coefficient presheaves.** An
418	isomorphism `e : F ≅ G` of presheaves of `𝒪_X`-modules induces an isomorphism of section {\v C}ech

423	    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (e : F ≅ G) (p : ℕ)
424	    (h : IsZero (cechCohomology U F p)) : IsZero (cechCohomology U G p) :=
425	  h.of_iso ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor Ab.{u} (ComplexShape.up ℕ) p).mapIso
426	    ((sectionCechComplexFunctor U).mapIso e)).symm

431	quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`, the affine structure theorem `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`
432	gives `F ≅ ~M` with `M = Γ(Spec R, F)`; transporting along this isomorphism

436	standard cover of a distinguished open `D(f)`), which is the change-of-base-to-`R_f` leaf. -/
437	theorem affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
438	    (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
439	    (htilde : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (f : R),
440	        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
441	          = ⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) →
442	        ∀ p, 0 < p →
443	          IsZero (cechCohomology
444	            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
445	            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
446	              (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p)) :
447	    HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystem R) F := by
448	  intro c hc p hp
449	  obtain ⟨n, g, f, rfl, hcov⟩ := hc
450	  refine cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso _
451	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).mapIso
452	      (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).symm) p ?_

468	    (htilde : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (f : R),
469	        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)
470	          = ⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) →
471	        ∀ p, 0 < p →
472	          IsZero (cechCohomology
473	            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
474	            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
475	              (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p))
476	    (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
477	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens)) F p) : e = 0 :=

479	    (affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing F htilde)
480	    ⊤ ⟨1, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one⟩ p hp e

\begin{lemma}[{\v C}ech cohomology transports along an isomorphism of coefficients]
  \label{lem:cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso}
  \uses{def:cech_cohomology_accessor, def:section_cech_functoriality}
  \textit{Project-local transport lemma.}
  Let \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) be an index family and let \(e : \mathcal{F} \cong
  \mathcal{G}\) be an isomorphism of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. If
  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for some \(p\), then
  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{G}) = 0\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:section_cech_functoriality}
  The section {\v C}ech complex is functorial in the coefficient presheaf
  (Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_functoriality}), so \(e\) induces an isomorphism of section
  {\v C}ech complexes \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \cong
  \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{G})\), and applying the degree-\(p\) homology
  functor yields an isomorphism \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \cong
  \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{G})\). A group isomorphic to a zero object is itself zero.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[A standard cover of \(D(f)\) spans the unit ideal of \(R_f\)]
  \label{lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affine_cover_span_localizationAway}
  \uses{lem:basicOpen_iSup_eq_top_iff_span}
  \textit{Project-local infrastructure.}
  Let \(R\) be a ring, \(f \in R\), and \(g : \iota \to R\) a finite family with \(D(f) =
  \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) in \(\operatorname{Spec} R\). Then the images
  \(\operatorname{algebraMap} R\, R_f\, (g_i)\) span the unit ideal of \(R_f =
  \operatorname{Localization.Away} f\):
  \[
    \operatorname{span}\bigl(\{\, g_i/1 : i \in \iota \,\}\bigr) = R_f.
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  Pull the equality \(D(f) = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) back along the canonical open immersion
  \(\operatorname{Spec} R_f \cong D(f) \hookrightarrow \operatorname{Spec} R\), whose underlying
  continuous map is the comap of \(\operatorname{algebraMap} R\, R_f\). Comap commutes with
  distinguished opens and with suprema, so the pullback of \(\bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) is
  \(\bigsqcup_i D(g_i/1)\), while the pullback of \(D(f)\) is all of \(\operatorname{Spec} R_f\)
  because \(f\) is a unit in \(R_f\). Thus \(\bigsqcup_i D(g_i/1) = \operatorname{Spec} R_f\), which
  by the span criterion (a family of distinguished opens covers the whole spectrum iff the elements
  generate the unit ideal) is equivalent to \(\operatorname{span}\{g_i/1\} = R_f\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Per-face section identification under change of base to \(R_f\)]
  \label{lem:tilde_section_changeOfBase}
  \uses{lem:modules_restrict_basicOpen, lem:tilde_section_isLocalizedModule}
  % TODO (prover): not yet a Lean declaration; the intended engine is QcohRestrictBasicOpen
  % (modulesRestrictBasicOpen / modulesRestrictBasicOpenIso / presentationModulesRestrictBasicOpen).
  \textit{Project-local building block.}
  Let \(R\) be a ring, \(M\) an \(R\)-module, \(f \in R\), and write \(R_f =
  \operatorname{Localization.Away} f\), \(M_f = M \otimes_R R_f\). For a tuple
  \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) of indices into a family \(g : \iota \to R\) with each
  \(D(g_i) \subseteq D(f)\), set \(g_\sigma = \prod_k g_{\sigma k}\). Then there is a natural
  isomorphism of abelian groups
  \[
    \Gamma\bigl(D_R(g_\sigma), \widetilde{M}\bigr)
      \;\cong\;
    \Gamma\bigl(D_{R_f}(g_\sigma/1), \widetilde{M_f}\bigr),
  \]
  both sides being canonically the away localisation \(M_{g_\sigma}\): since \(D(g_\sigma) \subseteq
  D(f)\), the element \(f\) is already invertible in \(R_{g_\sigma}\), so \(M_{g_\sigma} =
  (M_f)_{g_\sigma}\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:modules_restrict_basicOpen, lem:tilde_section_isLocalizedModule}
  By Lemma~\ref{lem:tilde_section_isLocalizedModule} the section group
  \(\Gamma(D_R(g_\sigma), \widetilde{M})\) is the away localisation \(M_{g_\sigma}\), and likewise
  \(\Gamma(D_{R_f}(g_\sigma/1), \widetilde{M_f}) = (M_f)_{g_\sigma}\). Restricting \(\widetilde{M}\)
  to \(D(f)\) and transporting along \(D(f) \cong \operatorname{Spec} R_f\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_restrict_basicOpen}) identifies \(\widetilde{M}|_{D(f)}\) with
  \(\widetilde{M_f}\). Because \(D(g_\sigma) \subseteq D(f)\), the structure map \(R \to
  R_{g_\sigma}\) factors through \(R_f\), so \(f\) maps to a unit and the canonical map
  \(M_{g_\sigma} \to (M_f)_{g_\sigma}\) is an isomorphism. Composing gives the claimed
  identification, natural in \(\sigma\) because all maps involved are restriction/localisation maps.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Change-of-base isomorphism of section {\v C}ech complexes]
  \label{lem:section_cech_changeOfBase_iso}
  \uses{lem:tilde_section_changeOfBase, def:section_cech_complex,
    def:section_cech_functoriality}
  % TODO (prover): not yet a Lean declaration; assembles lem:tilde_section_changeOfBase degreewise
  % using the QcohRestrictBasicOpen engine.
  \textit{Project-local building block.}
  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:tilde_section_changeOfBase}, the per-face identifications
  assemble into an isomorphism of cochain complexes of abelian groups
  \[
    \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet\bigl(\{D_R(g_i)\}, \widetilde{M}\bigr)
      \;\cong\;
    \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet\bigl(\{D_{R_f}(g_i/1)\}, \widetilde{M_f}\bigr).
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tilde_section_changeOfBase}
  In each cohomological degree \(p\) the section {\v C}ech group is the product over tuples
  \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) of the face section groups \(\Gamma(D(g_\sigma),
  \widetilde{M})\); apply the per-face isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:tilde_section_changeOfBase}
  factorwise to get a degreewise isomorphism. The {\v C}ech differentials are alternating sums of
  restriction (= localisation) maps, and the per-face isomorphisms are themselves localisation maps,
  so the two commute: the degreewise isomorphisms form a chain map, hence an isomorphism of cochain
  complexes.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Standard subcover {\v C}ech vanishing for the tilde sheaf (the residual)]
  \label{lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover}
  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway,
    lem:tilde_section_changeOfBase, lem:section_cech_changeOfBase_iso,
    def:cech_cohomology_accessor}
  % TODO (prover): not yet a Lean declaration; this is the residual `htilde` that the reduced forms
  % `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing` / `affine_serre_vanishing_of_tildeVanishing`
  % take as hypothesis.
  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover
  % {\v C}ech vanishing on an affine open) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L44--52).
  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
  % Let $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$ be a standard
  % open covering of an affine open of $X$.
  % Then $\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for
  % all $p > 0$."
  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}.}
  Let \(R\) be a ring, \(M\) an \(R\)-module, \(f \in R\), and \(g : \{1, \ldots, n\} \to R\) a finite
  family with \(D(f) = \bigsqcup_i D(g_i)\) in \(\operatorname{Spec} R\). Then for every \(p > 0\),
  \[
    \check{H}^p\bigl(\{D(g_i)\}, \widetilde{M}\bigr) = 0.
  \]
\end{lemma}

% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Write $U = \Spec(A)$ for some ring $A$.
% In other words, $f_1, \ldots, f_n$ are elements of $A$
% which generate the unit ideal of $A$.
% Write $\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}$ for some $A$-module $M$.
% Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
% $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
% is identified with the complex
% $\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to \ldots$"
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway,
    lem:tilde_section_changeOfBase, lem:section_cech_changeOfBase_iso}
  Set \(R_f = \operatorname{Localization.Away} f\) and \(M_f = M \otimes_R R_f\). Following the Stacks
  proof's ``Write \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\)'' step, we transfer the whole computation to
  \(\operatorname{Spec} R_f\). By Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_changeOfBase_iso} the section {\v C}ech
  complex of \(\widetilde{M}\) over \(\{D_R(g_i)\}\) is isomorphic, as a cochain complex of abelian
  groups, to the section {\v C}ech complex of \(\widetilde{M_f}\) over \(\{D_{R_f}(g_i/1)\}\) (the
  per-face identifications of Lemma~\ref{lem:tilde_section_changeOfBase}, assembled degreewise).

  Over \(R_f\) the family \(\{g_i/1\}\) spans the unit ideal
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_cover_span_localizationAway}), so \(\{D_{R_f}(g_i/1)\}\) is a standard cover
  of \emph{all} of \(\operatorname{Spec} R_f\). The whole-space standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} therefore applies to \(\widetilde{M_f}\) and kills its positive
  homology: \(\check{H}^p(\{D_{R_f}(g_i/1)\}, \widetilde{M_f}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\). Transporting back
  along the cochain isomorphism gives \(\check{H}^p(\{D_R(g_i)\}, \widetilde{M}) = 0\) for all
  \(p > 0\).
\end{proof}
